title: MacDowell Fellowship
description: MacDowell awards two-to-eight-week residencies to professional artists working in architecture, film/video, interdisciplinary arts, literature, music composition, theatre, and visual arts, at its campus in Peterborough, New Hampshire. There is no residency fee; a private studio, accommodations, and three prepared meals a day are provided at no cost, and need-based stipends plus travel reimbursement grants (up to $2,500) are available to fellows who require them. Applications for Spring/Summer 2027 residencies (roughly March-August 2027) are due September 10, 2026.
visaNote: The fellowship is an unpaid residency grant, not employment; international fellows typically enter the U.S. on a visitor visa or ESTA and should confirm current entry requirements with MacDowell and their nearest U.S. consulate.

title: Yaddo Artist Residency
description: Yaddo hosts professional artists -- visual artists, writers, composers, choreographers, filmmakers, and performers -- for two-week to two-month residencies on its estate in Saratoga Springs, New York. Room, board, and studio space are provided at no charge, and modest access grants help offset travel costs for admitted artists who need them. The Winter application cycle, covering residencies from roughly May 2027 through March 2028, closes December 20, 2026.
visaNote: The residency is an unpaid grant, not employment; international artists typically enter the U.S. on a visitor visa or ESTA and should confirm current entry requirements with Yaddo and their nearest U.S. consulate.

title: Ucross Foundation Artist Residency
description: Ucross hosts visual artists, writers, composers, choreographers, and interdisciplinary artists for two-to-six-week residencies on its ranch in Clearmont, Wyoming, providing private living quarters, chef-prepared meals, and studio space at no charge -- there is no residency fee. Accepted artists also receive a $1,500 stipend at orientation to help defray travel costs. The Fall 2027 residency application (for stays roughly August-December 2027) opens November 1, 2026 and closes January 15, 2027.
visaNote: The residency stipend defrays travel costs and is not employment income; international artists typically enter the U.S. on a visitor visa or ESTA and should confirm current entry requirements with Ucross and their nearest U.S. consulate.
